description: What should the demo dashboard display as its primary view — a real-time settlement feed, a customer balance overview, or an agent package leaderboard?
options: Settlement feed view: real-time list of TaskReceipts as they appear on-chain, showing quality-gated splits — best for demonstrating the core billing flow to judges
Customer dashboard view: balance, deposit/withdraw actions, spending history, and caps — best for showing the customer experience
Agent marketplace view: registered packages with earnings, success rates, and author stats — best for demonstrating the marketplace angle
Split-panel view combining settlement feed + customer balance — covers both angles but more complex to build
rationale: The dashboard is the visual anchor for the demo video. Its primary view determines what story the hackathon submission tells to judges. A settlement feed emphasizes verifiable billing; a customer dashboard emphasizes UX; a marketplace view emphasizes the ecosystem play. This is a product/narrative decision that should be made by the team, not an individual agent.

description: What visual style should the demo dashboard adopt — Solana ecosystem aesthetic, CTO/5D Labs brand continuity, or a neutral developer tool look?
options: Solana-branded aesthetic: dark theme with Solana purple/green gradients, ecosystem-familiar patterns — appeals to Solana hackathon judges
CTO/5D Labs brand continuity: match existing cto-web styling and design tokens — professional, consistent with the platform
Developer tool aesthetic: minimal, terminal-inspired with monospace fonts and high-contrast data display — emphasizes technical credibility
rationale: The hackathon context creates a tension between brand consistency (CTO already has a web presence) and ecosystem signaling (Solana judges expect Solana-native aesthetics). This is a brand/positioning decision that affects how judges perceive the project.

description: Which component library should the demo dashboard use?
options: shadcn/ui with Tailwind CSS — popular in the ecosystem, highly customizable, tree-shakeable
Chakra UI — good defaults, fast to build with, but heavier bundle
Plain Tailwind with minimal custom components — lightest weight for a single-page demo dashboard
rationale: The existing cto-web service is already deployed (visible in infrastructure context). If it uses a specific component library, the dashboard should match for consistency. If not, the choice affects build speed vs. visual quality for a hackathon demo that needs to look polished but ships fast.
